name: bulbous water dispenser
description: This device almost looks like an earthly water cooler, but the base is made of scrap metal, and the tank is a bulbous, black glass globe that obfuscates its contents.  It has no tap; instead, the base has a small compartment with a plastic flap, resembling that of a vending machine.  Examine it to dispense drinks, if it has any.

effect.title: You see a seven-segment LED display and a circular button.  The display reads: <u_val:nl_safehouse_water_stock>

name: clouded cylinder
description: A massive cylindrical device.  Perhaps the exterior was once translucent, but it's now deeply clouded and yellowed, with filth grasping where the plastic-like material meets the floor and ceiling.  Behind the plastic is some sort of large, bulbous object, too obscured to make heads or tails of.  It's bolted down with extremely thick and heavy-looking hardware.

name: otherworldly boiler
description: A huge, pot-bellied device that resembles something between a coal-burning stove and a boiler, with a wide, hinged cage covering the combustion chamber.  Three unmarked gauges sit like alien eyes upon its face.  A side-mounted crank is its only visible control.  In defiance of its apparent function, it has no exhaust vent; in its place is a hose twisted on itself several times, coiled in copper, and terminating in a bulbous back-mounted battery pack.

name: twitchy medical robot
description: A lanky set of robotic limbs armed with surgical devices and construction tools.  The arms protrude from a barrel-like base, mounted upon small casters.  Even when idle, it moves like an insect grooming its antennae.  The scalpel is stropped against another arm.  The over-articulated grasper picks at the other tools.  The circular saw silently rotates, as if it were itself scanning the surroundings.  Embedded in the side of the device is a bulbous computer terminal, displaying several options, from standard medical services to advanced bionic surgery.

name: operation table
description: A hard metal table on wheels, with several auxiliary medical devices of unknown origin.  Several screens dangle from the edge, black in their idle state, but with several burned-in lines, as if it had seen continuous service for many years.
